examples.title: Speed Formula
examples.content: Speed = distance/time. In symbols: [L T⁻¹]. This shorthand is the dimensional formula for speed.

examples.title: Newton (Force Unit)
examples.content: Newton = kg·m/s² = [M L T⁻²]. The dimensional formula shows what base units make a Newton.

examples.title: Joule (Energy Unit)
examples.content: Joule = kg·m²/s² = [M L² T⁻²]. The dimensional formula tells you exactly what base units are 'inside' one Joule.
